generatedSentence: In a whimsical `habitat`, `nymphs` with `fluffy` `hairstyle` tended a `vineyard`, their `goggles` reflecting the `universal` `enjoyment`. `Pachyderms` were `munching` `omelet` from `vases` while `monkeys` played `okay` on old `keypads`. Their `makers`, simple folk in `cabins`, knew the `odor` of fresh `cotton` and `cowhide`. A `peacock` strutted, an `indicator` of the `utopic` calm, even as a `caterpillar` dreamed of transformation, `indulge`ing in `watering` can droplets. But a `virulent` `tyranny` began to `fortify` its hold, led by a fearsome `tyrant` whose `affrays` and `vendetta` threatened the `antiquated` `aesthetic`. `Outlaws` `lurking` in `greywackes` `overlooking` the `bank` had `slacked` their vigilance, allowing the `unexplained` `doomsday` plot to fester. The `narrator` spoke of a `dilemma`: `eloping` from the `cutoff` `locker` or facing the `awfully` grim `failure`. A reclusive `veejay`, with `sunglasses` perpetually on, started `validating` encrypted messages through a `valve`. The `battlefield` was set; `vikings` and `alligator` `homunculus` `crowned` with `gandiose` intent faced the `ultimate` `odds`. Our `vigilant` hero, `jabbed` with `oxygen` for `vital`ity, `ducked` the `dragon`'s fire, a maneuver taught by ancient `jujitsu` `fathers`. This `reliever` of worlds, with a `leg`ion of `eagles` at their side, understood the `synergy` needed to move `upwards`. This was it, the `other` side of the `overlay`, the climactic `doomed` confrontation. After the `evaluation` and `hospital` visits, a grand `reunion` was held. The `dishwasher` was finally fixed, and the `waffle` iron hummed. A `vaccine` for the lingering `virulent` effects was delivered via the `mailbox`, a `tangible` sign of hope. The children, now `kinder`, watched as the `kelp` gardens bloomed again. No more `peepee` jokes, only `unanimous` `enjoyment` and `kissing` away old fears. The `hacking` was `snap`ped, the `headset` hung, a new peace `cajoles` the land.
panelSentences: In a whimsical `habitat`, `nymphs` with `fluffy` `hairstyle` tended a `vineyard`, their `goggles` reflecting the `universal` `enjoyment`. `Pachyderms` were `munching` `omelet` from `vases` while `monkeys` played `okay` on old `keypads`. Their `makers`, simple folk in `cabins`, knew the `odor` of fresh `cotton` and `cowhide`. A `peacock` strutted, an `indicator` of the `utopic` calm, even as a `caterpillar` dreamed of transformation, `indulge`ing in `watering` can droplets.

But a `virulent` `tyranny` began to `fortify` its hold, led by a fearsome `tyrant` whose `affrays` and `vendetta` threatened the `antiquated` `aesthetic`. `Outlaws` `lurking` in `greywackes` `overlooking` the `bank` had `slacked` their vigilance, allowing the `unexplained` `doomsday` plot to fester. The `narrator` spoke of a `dilemma`: `eloping` from the `cutoff` `locker` or facing the `awfully` grim `failure`. A reclusive `veejay`, with `sunglasses` perpetually on, started `validating` encrypted messages through a `valve`.

The `battlefield` was set; `vikings` and `alligator` `homunculus` `crowned` with `gandiose` intent faced the `ultimate` `odds`. Our `vigilant` hero, `jabbed` with `oxygen` for `vital`ity, `ducked` the `dragon`'s fire, a maneuver taught by ancient `jujitsu` `fathers`. This `reliever` of worlds, with a `leg`ion of `eagles` at their side, understood the `synergy` needed to move `upwards`. This was it, the `other` side of the `overlay`, the climactic `doomed` confrontation.

After the `evaluation` and `hospital` visits, a grand `reunion` was held. The `dishwasher` was finally fixed, and the `waffle` iron hummed. A `vaccine` for the lingering `virulent` effects was delivered via the `mailbox`, a `tangible` sign of hope. The children, now `kinder`, watched as the `kelp` gardens bloomed again. No more `peepee` jokes, only `unanimous` `enjoyment` and `kissing` away old fears. The `hacking` was `snap`ped, the `headset` hung, a new peace `cajoles` the land.
rawPanelSentences: In a whimsical `habitat`, `nymphs` with `fluffy` `hairstyle` tended a `vineyard`, their `goggles` reflecting the `universal` `enjoyment`. `Pachyderms` were `munching` `omelet` from `vases` while `monkeys` played `okay` on old `keypads`. Their `makers`, simple folk in `cabins`, knew the `odor` of fresh `cotton` and `cowhide`. A `peacock` strutted, an `indicator` of the `utopic` calm, even as a `caterpillar` dreamed of transformation, `indulge`ing in `watering` can droplets.

But a `virulent` `tyranny` began to `fortify` its hold, led by a fearsome `tyrant` whose `affrays` and `vendetta` threatened the `antiquated` `aesthetic`. `Outlaws` `lurking` in `greywackes` `overlooking` the `bank` had `slacked` their vigilance, allowing the `unexplained` `doomsday` plot to fester. The `narrator` spoke of a `dilemma`: `eloping` from the `cutoff` `locker` or facing the `awfully` grim `failure`. A reclusive `veejay`, with `sunglasses` perpetually on, started `validating` encrypted messages through a `valve`.

The `battlefield` was set; `vikings` and `alligator` `homunculus` `crowned` with `gandiose` intent faced the `ultimate` `odds`. Our `vigilant` hero, `jabbed` with `oxygen` for `vital`ity, `ducked` the `dragon`'s fire, a maneuver taught by ancient `jujitsu` `fathers`. This `reliever` of worlds, with a `leg`ion of `eagles` at their side, understood the `synergy` needed to move `upwards`. This was it, the `other` side of the `overlay`, the climactic `doomed` confrontation.

After the `evaluation` and `hospital` visits, a grand `reunion` was held. The `dishwasher` was finally fixed, and the `waffle` iron hummed. A `vaccine` for the lingering `virulent` effects was delivered via the `mailbox`, a `tangible` sign of hope. The children, now `kinder`, watched as the `kelp` gardens bloomed again. No more `peepee` jokes, only `unanimous` `enjoyment` and `kissing` away old fears. The `hacking` was `snap`ped, the `headset` hung, a new peace `cajoles` the land.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a female warrior with a glowing cyberpunk katana and a chrome visor that reflects digital runes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a tranquil, overgrown cyber-garden, observing glowing flora, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist confronts a monstrous, shadowy figure with digital glitches, close-up, dramatic low-angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist mid-air, deflecting energy blasts with her katana, dynamic action shot from above. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist stands victorious atop a ruined cityscape, looking towards a new dawn, medium shot.

definitions.definition: The act of suddenly disturbing anyone; an assault or attack.
A tumultuous assault or quarrel.
The fighting of two or more persons, in a public place, to the terror of others.
definitions.example: The affray in the busy marketplace caused great terror and disorder.

definitions.definition: Terror.
To startle from quiet; to alarm.
To frighten; to scare; to frighten away.
A fugitive from the law.
(history) A criminal who is excluded from normal legal rights; one who can be killed at will without legal penalty.
A person who operates outside established norms.
definitions.example: The main character in the play was a bit of an outlaw who refused to shake hands or say thank you.

definitions.definition: A wild horse.
An in-law: a relative by marriage.
One who would be an in-law except that the marriage-like relationship is unofficial.
A prostitute who works alone, without a pimp.
To declare illegal.
To place a ban upon.
To remove from legal jurisdiction or enforcement.
definitions.example: to outlaw a debt or claim

definitions.definition: To deprive of legal force.
definitions.example: Laws outlawed by necessity. — Fuller.

definitions.definition: (diminutive) An odd number.
definitions.example: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.

definitions.definition: Something left over, not forming part of a set.
The ratio of the probability of an event happening to that of it not happening.
definitions.example: I'd say the odds are strongly in favor of the sun rising tomorrow morning.

definitions.definition: The ratio of winnings to stake in betting situations.
(Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
A young girl, especially one who is attractive, beautiful or graceful.
The larva of certain insects.
Any of various butterflies of the family Nymphalidae.
A usurper; one who gains power and rules extralegally, distinguished from kings elevated by election or succession.
Any monarch or governor.
A despot; a ruler who governs unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
(by extension) Any person who abuses the power of position or office to treat others unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
(by extension) A villain; a person or thing who uses strength or violence to treat others unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
The tyrant bird, members of the family Tyrannidae, which often fight or drive off other birds which approach their nests.
To act like a tyrant; to be tyrannical.
To tyrannize.
Tyrannical, tyrannous; like, characteristic of, or in the manner of a tyrant.
Someone who makes; a person or thing that makes or produces something.
(usually capitalized and preceded by the) God.
A poet.
Someone who signs a promissory note, thereby becoming responsible for payment.
To chew with a grinding, crunching sound, and with the mouth closed — often used with on.
definitions.example: Jim was munching on a biscotti.

definitions.definition: To eat vigorously or with excitement.
definitions.example: Watching old Bill munch his pancakes makes me hungry!

definitions.definition: The sound or action of one who munches.

Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
Attestation of a fact or event; testimony.
definitions.example: She can bear witness, since she was there at the time.

definitions.definition: One who sees or has personal knowledge of something.
definitions.example: As a witness to the event, I can confirm that he really said that.

definitions.definition: Someone called to give evidence in a court.
definitions.example: The witness for the prosecution did not seem very credible.

definitions.definition: One who is called upon to witness an event or action, such as a wedding or the signing of a document.
definitions.example: The bridesmaid and best man at a wedding typically serve as the witnesses.

definitions.definition: Something that serves as evidence; a sign or token.
To furnish proof of, to show.
definitions.example: 1667: round he throws his baleful eyes / That witness'd huge affliction and dismay — John Milton, Paradise Lost, Book 1 ll. 56-7

definitions.definition: To take as evidence.
To see or gain knowledge of through experience.
definitions.example: He witnessed the accident.

definitions.definition: (construed with to or for) To present personal religious testimony; to preach at (someone) or on behalf of.
To see the execution of (a legal instrument), and subscribe it for the purpose of establishing its authenticity.
definitions.example: to witness a bond or a deed

definitions.definition: A small dwelling characteristic of the frontier, especially when built from logs with simple tools and not constructed by professional builders, but by those who meant to live in it.
definitions.example: Abraham Lincoln was born in a log cabin.

definitions.definition: A chalet or lodge, especially one that can hold large groups of people.
A private room on a ship.
definitions.example: the captain's cabin:  Passengers shall remain in their cabins.

definitions.definition: The interior of a boat, enclosed to create a small room, particularly for sleeping.
The passenger area of an airplane.
(travel) The section of a passenger plane having the same class of service.
A signal box.
A small room; an enclosed place.
(Indian English) A private office; particularly of a doctor, businessman, lawyer, or other professional.
The point at which something terminates or to which it is limited.
A road, path or channel that provides a shorter or quicker path; a shortcut.
A device that stops the flow of a current.
A device for saving steam by regulating its admission to the cylinder (see quotation at cut-off).
A cessation in a flow or activity.
The player who acts directly before the player on the button pre-flop.
(chiefly in the plural) shorts made by cutting off the legs from trousers
A horizontal line separating sections of the page.
Constituting a limit or ending.
Designating a score or value demarcating the presence (or absence) of a disease, condition, or similar.
A member of the obsolete taxonomic order Pachydermata, grouping of thick-skinned, hoofed animals such as the rhinoceros, hippopotamus, elephant, pig and horse.
An elephant
A person with thick skin; someone who is not affected by or does not care what others say about him or her.
Someone who is insensitive.
A small board with keys primarily used for tactile input into a machine.
definitions.example: Many laptop computers have special function keys which turn part of the alphabetical keyboard into a numeric keypad.

definitions.definition: A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
definitions.example: The vineyard of Château Margaux stands as the producer of one of the world's greatest and most sought-after red wines.

definitions.definition: One of the Scandinavian or other Northern European seafaring warriors that raided (and then settled) the British Isles and other parts of Europe in the 8th to the 11th centuries and, according to many historians, were the first Europeans to reach North America.
(by extension) A stock character common in the fantasy genre, namely a barbarian, generally equipped with an axe or sword and a helmet adorned with horns.
A Norseman (mediaeval Scandinavian).
(mildly) An ethnic Swede, Norwegian, Dane, Icelander or Faroe Islander.
A player on the Minnesota Vikings NFL team.
An upright open container used mainly for displaying fresh, dried, or artificial flowers.
The body of the Corinthian capital.
A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a pipe.
A device that admits fuel and air into the cylinder of an internal combustion engine, or one that allows combustion gases to exit.
One or more membranous partitions, flaps, or folds, which permit the passage of the contents of a vessel or cavity in one direction, but stop or control the flow in the opposite direction
definitions.example: the ileocolic, mitral, and semilunar valves

definitions.definition: One of the leaves of a folding-door, or a window-sash.
A vacuum tube.
One of the pieces into which certain fruits naturally separate when they dehisce.
A small portion of certain anthers, which opens like a trapdoor to allow the pollen to escape, such as in the barberry.
One of the pieces or divisions of bivalve or multivalve shells.
One of the two similar portions of the shell of a diatom.
To control (flow) by means of a valve.
To render valid.
To check or prove the validity of; verify.
To have its validity successfully proven.
definitions.example: The data file cannot be imported because it doesn't validate.

definitions.definition: Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
To work as a video jockey; to present videos.
Protective eyewear set in a flexible frame to fit snugly against the face.
definitions.example: Goggles must be worn in the swimming pool.

definitions.definition: Blinds for shying horses.
The process or act of reuniting.
definitions.example: It was a tearful and heartfelt reunion as the trapped miners finally saw their dear loved ones again.

definitions.definition: A planned event at which members of a dispersed group meet together.
definitions.example: Are you going to the reunion this year?

definitions.definition: An assessment, such as an annual personnel performance review used as the basis for a salary increase or bonus, or a summary of a particular situation.
A completion of a mathematical operation; a valuation.
Determination of the value of a variable or expression.
An institution where one can place and borrow money and take care of financial affairs.
A branch office of such an institution.
An underwriter or controller of a card game; also banque.
A fund from deposits or contributions, to be used in transacting business; a joint stock or capital.
The sum of money etc. which the dealer or banker has as a fund from which to draw stakes and pay losses.
Money; profit
In certain games, such as dominos, a fund of pieces from which the players are allowed to draw.
A safe and guaranteed place of storage for and retrieval of important items or goods.
definitions.example: blood bank; sperm bank; data bank

definitions.definition: A device used to store coins or currency.
definitions.example: If you want to buy a bicycle, you need to put the money in your piggy bank.

definitions.definition: To deal with a bank or financial institution, or for an institution to provide financial services to a client.
definitions.example: He banked with Barclays.

definitions.definition: To put into a bank.
definitions.example: I'm going to bank the money.

definitions.definition: To conceal in the rectum for use in prison.
definitions.example: Johnny banked some coke for me.

definitions.definition: Not explained. Of unknown cause or origin.
A characteristic or property that particular things have in common.
Of or pertaining to the universe.
Common to all members of a group or class.
Common to all society; worldwide
definitions.example: She achieved universal fame.

definitions.definition: Unlimited; vast; infinite
Useful for many purposes; all-purpose.
definitions.example: universal wrench

definitions.definition: Towards a higher place; towards what is above.
To a higher figure or amount.
Towards something which is higher in order, larger, superior etc.
Backwards in time, into the past.
To or into later life.
The most basic or fundamental of a set of things
The final or most distant point; the conclusion
The greatest extremity; the maximum
A non-contact competitive team sport played with a 175 gram flying disc, the object of which is to score points by passing the disc to a player in the opposing end zone; ultimate frisbee.
To finish; to complete.
Final; last in a series.
(of a syllable) Last in a word or other utterance.
Being the greatest possible; maximum; most extreme.
definitions.example: the ultimate disappointment

definitions.definition: Being the most distant or extreme; farthest.
That will happen at some time; eventual.
Last in a train of progression or consequences; tended toward by all that precedes; arrived at, as the last result; final.
Incapable of further analysis; incapable of further division or separation; constituent; elemental.
definitions.example: an ultimate constituent of matter

definitions.definition: A pair of headphones or earphones, or a singular headphone or earphone, typically with an attached microphone.
Any electronic device worn on the head.
On a bicycle, the system of bearings that connects the fork to the frame.
Someone or something that has a fluffy texture.
A person who is superficial, who lacks depth or seriousness.
A babycino (frothy milk drink).
Covered with fluff.
Light; soft; airy.
Warm and comforting.
Not clearly defined or explained; fuzzy.
Lightweight; superficial; lacking depth or seriousness.
To remain concealed in order to ambush.
To remain unobserved.
To hang out or wait around a location, preferably without drawing attention to oneself.
To view an internet forum without posting comments.
To saddle (a person) with an undesirable task or duty.
The act of one who lurks.
Tinted glasses worn to protect the eyes from the sun.
A person wearing sunglasses
Badly, terribly.
definitions.example: She led after the swiming and cycling, but ran awfully and came in fourth.

definitions.definition: Very; exceedingly; extremely; excessively.
In a manner inspiring awe.
Reverently.
Fearfully.

In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).
definitions.example: A cat is an animal, not a plant. Humans are also animals, under the scientific definition, as we are not plants.

definitions.definition: In non-scientific usage, any member of the kingdom Animalia other than a human.
In non-scientific usage, any land-living vertebrate (i.e. not fishes, insects, etc.).
A person who behaves wildly; a bestial, brutal, brutish, cruel, or inhuman person.
definitions.example: My students are animals.

definitions.definition: A person of a particular type.
definitions.example: He's a political animal.

definitions.definition: Matter, thing.
definitions.example: a whole different animal

definitions.definition: Either of two species of large amphibious reptile, Alligator mississippiensis or Alligator sinensis, in the genus Alligator within order Crocodilia, which have sharp teeth and very strong jaws and are native to the Americas and China, respectively.
definitions.example: All you could see of the alligator were its two eyes above the water, and suddenly it snatched up and caught the poor bird with its strong jaws full of sharp teeth.

definitions.definition: Dwarf crocodile (Osteolaemus tetraspis)
Any of various machines with strong jaws, one of which opens like the movable jaw of an alligator.
Any of various vehicles that have relatively long, low noses in front of a cab or other, usually windowed, structure.
(of paint or other coatings) To crack in a pattern resembling an alligator's skin.
Used in a common chronometric counting scheme, in which each iteration is sequentially numbered and supposed to be approximately one second in length.
An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
See other (determiner) below
Second.
definitions.example: I get paid every other week.

definitions.definition: Alien.
Different.
Left, as opposed to right.
Apart from; in the phrase "other than".
definitions.example: Other than that, I'm fine.

definitions.definition: Otherwise.
definitions.example: If you think other. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: A piece of paper pasted upon the tympan sheet to improve the impression by making it stronger at a particular place.
(betting) Odds which are set higher than expected or warranted. Favorable odds.
A horse going off at higher odds than it appears to warrant, based on its past performances.
A decal attached to a computer keyboard to relabel the keys.
A block of program code that is loaded over something previously loaded, so as to replace the functionality.
A pop-up covering an existing part of the display.
A cravat.
To lay, spread, or apply something over or across; cover.
To overwhelm; to press excessively upon.
To lie over (someone, especially a child) in order to smother it; to suffocate.
To put an overlay on.
To offer a view (of something) from a higher position.
definitions.example: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.

definitions.definition: To fail to notice; to look over and beyond (anything) without seeing it.
definitions.example: These errors were overlooked by the proofreaders.

definitions.definition: To pretend not to have noticed (something, especially a mistake or flaw); to pass over (something) without censure or punishment.
definitions.example: I’m not willing to overlook such bad behaviour.

definitions.definition: To look down upon (something) from a place that is over or above.
definitions.example: to overlook a valley from a hill

definitions.definition: To supervise, oversee; to watch over.
definitions.example: to overlook a gang of laborers

definitions.definition: To observe or watch (someone or something) surreptitiously or secretly.
To inspect (something); to examine; to look over carefully or repeatedly.
To look upon with an evil eye; to bewitch by looking upon; to fascinate.
To fail to care for or attend to something.
definitions.example: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts

definitions.definition: To omit to notice; to forbear to treat with attention or respect; to slight.
definitions.example: to neglect strangers

definitions.definition: To fail to do or carry out something due to oversight or carelessness.
Suffering from neglect.
One who narrates or tells stories.
The person or the "voice" whose viewpoint is used in telling a story.
The person providing the voice-over in a documentary.
A government in which a single ruler (a tyrant) has absolute power; this system of government.
The office or jurisdiction of an absolute ruler.
Absolute power, or its use.
A system of government in which power is exercised on behalf of the ruler or ruling class, without regard to the wishes of the governed.
Extreme severity or rigour.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The brunch was waffles with strawberries and whipped cream.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
The larva of a butterfly or moth; leafworm.
definitions.example: The bird just ate that green caterpillar.

definitions.definition: A vehicle with a caterpillar track; a crawler.
To persuade someone to do something which they are reluctant to do, especially by flattery or promises; to coax.
To place a crown on the head of.
To formally declare (someone) a king, queen, emperor, etc.
To bestow something upon as a mark of honour, dignity, or recompense; to adorn; to dignify.
To form the topmost or finishing part of; to complete; to consummate; to perfect.
To declare (someone) a winner.
Of a baby, during the birthing process; for the surface of the baby's head to appear in the vaginal opening.
definitions.example: The mother was in the second stage of labor and the fetus had just crowned, prompting a round of encouragement from the midwives.

definitions.definition: To cause to round upward; to make anything higher at the middle than at the edges, such as the face of a machine pulley.
To hit on the head.
To shoot an opponent in the back of the head with a shotgun in a first-person shooter video game.
In checkers, to stack two checkers to indicate that the piece has become a king.
definitions.example: “Crown me!” I said, as I moved my checker to the back row.

definitions.definition: To widen the opening of the barrel.
To effect a lodgment upon, as upon the crest of the glacis, or the summit of the breach.
To lay the ends of the strands of (a knot) over and under each other.
Wearing a crown.
Great; excessive; supreme.
A pointer or index that indicates something.
A meter or gauge.
The needle or dial on such a meter.
Any of many substances, such as litmus, used to indicate the concentration of a substance, or the degree of a reaction.
A plant or animal whose presence is indicative of some specific environment.
A measure, such as unemployment rate, which can be used to predict economic trends.
A turn signal; each of the flashing lights on each side of a vehicle which indicate a turn is being made to left or right, or a lane change etc.
A bird, the honeyguide.
A male peafowl, especially Pavo cristatus, notable for its brilliant iridescently ocellated tail.
A peafowl (of the genus Pavo or Afropavo), either male or female.
A vainglorious person .
Any of various Asian species of papilionid butterflies of the genus Papilio.
To strut about proudly.
To engage in peacocking, ostentatious dress or behaviour to impress women.
Having a benevolent, courteous, friendly, generous, gentle, liberal, sympathetic, or warm-hearted nature or disposition, marked by consideration for – and service to – others.
Affectionate.
definitions.example: a kind man; a kind heart

definitions.definition: Favorable.
Mild, gentle, forgiving
definitions.example: The years have been kind to Richard Gere; he ages well.

definitions.definition: Gentle; tractable; easily governed.
definitions.example: a horse kind in harness

definitions.definition: Characteristic of the species; belonging to one's nature; natural; native.
Any of several large brown seaweeds (order Laminariales).
The calcined ashes of seaweed, formerly used in glass and iodine manufacture.
(of a disease or disease-causing agent) Highly infectious, malignant or deadly.
Hostile to the point of being venomous; intensely acrimonious.
definitions.example: The politicians were virulent in their hatred of the president.

definitions.definition: A substance given to stimulate the body's production of antibodies and provide immunity against a disease without causing the disease itself in the treatment, prepared from the agent that causes the disease (or a related, also effective, but safer disease), or a synthetic substitute.
Watchful, especially for danger or disorder; alert; wary
definitions.example: Be vigilant for signs of disease in your garden.

definitions.definition: A hard dark sandstone with poorly sorted angular grains of quartz, feldspar, and small rock fragments in a compact, clay-fine matrix.
A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
The day when God is expected to judge the world; end times.
Judgement day; the day of the Final Judgment; any day of decisive judgement or final dissolution.
Concerned with or predicting future universal destruction.
Given to or marked by forebodings or predictions of impending calamity.
Capable of causing widespread or total destruction.
Something which relieves (pain, etc.).
A relief pitcher.
definitions.example: The first reliever got the last two outs of the sixth.

definitions.definition: Someone who fills in for another.
definitions.example: I'll be the reliever today so that you can each take a break.

definitions.definition: A garment kept for being lent out.
Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
A gold coin with a face value of ten dollars, formerly used in the United States.
A 13th-century coin minted in Europe and circulated in England as a debased sterling silver penny, outlawed under Edward I.
A score of two under par for a hole.
To score an eagle.
The condition of enjoying anything.
definitions.example: Few activities better the enjoyment of a hearty meal eaten in good company.

definitions.definition: An enjoyable state of mind.
An activity that gives pleasure.
The exercise of a legal right.
Seeming to originate in utopia; utopian
(usually countable) A tree, Antiaris toxicaria, of the mulberry family, common in the forests of Java and the neighboring islands, with poisonous secretions.
A virulent poison used in Java and the adjacent islands for poisoning arrows derived from the tree.
A poison prepared from the climbing plant Strychnos tieute.
Based on unanimity, assent or agreement.
definitions.example: The debate went on for hours, but in the end the decision was unanimous.

definitions.definition: Sharing the same views or opinions, and being in harmony or accord.
definitions.example: We were unanimous: the President had to go.

definitions.definition: To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).
To be exposed or offered or to common use for hire; to turn prostitute.
To live the life of a drudge or hack.
To use as a hack; to let out for hire.
To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.
To play hackeysack.
Short and interrupted, broken, jerky; hacky.
definitions.example: A hacking cough. A hacking laugh. A hacking breath. A hacking cry.

definitions.definition: A (generally human) male who begets a child.
definitions.example: My father was a strong influence on me.

definitions.definition: A male ancestor more remote than a parent; a progenitor; especially, a first ancestor.
A term of respectful address for an elderly man.
definitions.example: Come, father; you can sit here.

definitions.definition: A term of respectful address for a priest.
A person who plays the role of a father in some way.
definitions.example: My brother was a father to me after my parents got divorced.

definitions.definition: The founder of a discipline or science.
definitions.example: Albert Einstein is the father of modern physics.

definitions.definition: Something that is the greatest or most significant of its kind.
Something inanimate that begets.
A senator of Ancient Rome.
To be a father to; to sire.
To give rise to.
To act as a father; to support and nurture.
To provide with a father.
To adopt as one's own.
State or condition of not meeting a desirable or intended objective, opposite of success.
An object, person or endeavour in a state of failure or incapable of success.
Termination of the ability of an item to perform its required function; breakdown.
Bankruptcy.
A type of storage compartment with a lock, usually used to store clothing, equipment, or books.
definitions.example: The student placed her books in her locker when she arrived at school.

definitions.definition: One who locks something.
definitions.example: The locker of the trapped chest must be careful, so as not to spring the trap.

definitions.definition: A locking differential.
A customs officer who guards a warehouse.
A quick breaking or cracking sound or the action of producing such a sound.
A sudden break.
An attempt to seize, bite, attack, or grab.
The act of making a snapping sound by pressing the thumb and an opposing finger of the same hand together and suddenly releasing the grip so that the finger hits against the palm.
A fastening device that makes a snapping sound when used.
A photograph; a snapshot.
definitions.example: We took a few snaps of the old church before moving on.

definitions.definition: The sudden release of something held under pressure or tension.
A thin circular cookie or similar baked good.
definitions.example: a ginger snap

definitions.definition: A brief, sudden period of a certain weather; used primarily in the phrase cold snap.
A very short period of time (figuratively, the time taken to snap one's fingers), or a task that can be accomplished in such a period.
definitions.example: I can fix most vacuum cleaners in a snap.

definitions.definition: A snap bean such as Phaseolus vulgaris.
A backward pass or handoff of a football from its position on the ground that puts the ball in play; a hike.
(somewhat colloquial) A rivet: a scrapbooking embellishment.
A small meal, a snack; lunch.
A card game, primarily for children, in which players cry "snap" to claim pairs of matching cards as they are turned up.
A greedy fellow.
That which is, or may be, snapped up; something bitten off, seized, or obtained by a single quick movement; hence, a bite, morsel, or fragment; a scrap.
Briskness; vigour; energy; decision
Any circumstance out of which money may be made or an advantage gained. used primarily in the phrase soft snap.
Something that is easy or effortless.
A snapper, or snap beetle.
Jounce (the fourth derivative of the position vector with respect to time), followed by crackle and pop
A quick offhand shot with a firearm; a snap shot.
Something of no value.
definitions.example: not worth a snap

definitions.definition: A visual message sent through the Snapchat application.
A crisp or pithy quality; epigrammatic point or force.
A tool used by riveters.
A tool used by glass-moulders.
A brief theatrical engagement.
An easy and profitable place or task; a sinecure.
A cheat or sharper.
To fracture or break apart suddenly.
definitions.example: He snapped his stick in anger.

definitions.definition: To give forth or produce a sharp cracking noise; to crack.
definitions.example: Blazing firewood snaps.

definitions.definition: To attempt to seize with the teeth or bite.
definitions.example: A dog snaps at a passenger. A fish snaps at the bait.

definitions.definition: To attempt to seize with eagerness.
definitions.example: She snapped at the chance to appear on television.

definitions.definition: To speak abruptly or sharply.
definitions.example: He snapped at me for the slightest mistake.

definitions.definition: To give way abruptly and loudly.
To suffer a mental breakdown, usually while under tension.
definitions.example: She should take a break before she snaps.

definitions.definition: To flash or appear to flash as with light.
To fit or fasten together with a snapping sound.
To jump to a fixed position relative to another element.
definitions.example: The floating toolbar will snap to the edge of the screen when dragged towards it.

definitions.definition: To snatch with or as if with the teeth.
To pull apart with a snapping sound; to pop loose.
To say abruptly or sharply.
To speak to abruptly or sharply; to treat snappishly; usually with up.
To cause something to emit a snapping sound.
definitions.example: to snap a fastener

definitions.definition: To close something using a snap as a fastener.

To snap one's fingers: to make a snapping sound, often by pressing the thumb and an opposing finger of the same hand together and suddenly releasing the grip so that the finger hits against the palm; alternatively, by bringing the index finger quickly down onto the middle finger and thumb.
To cause to move suddenly and smartly.
To take a photograph; to release a camera's shutter (which may make a snapping sound).
definitions.example: He snapped a picture of me with my mouth open and my eyes closed.

definitions.definition: To put (a football) in play by a backward pass or handoff from its position on the ground; to hike (a football).
definitions.example: He can snap the ball to a back twenty yards behind him.

definitions.definition: To misfire.
definitions.example: The gun snapped.

definitions.definition: To catch out sharply (a batsman who has just snicked a bowled ball).
Done, made, performed, etc., quickly and unexpectedly, or without deliberation.
definitions.example: a snap judgment or decision    a snap political convention

definitions.definition: The cry used in a game of snap when winning a hand.
By extension from the card game, "I've got one the same!", "Me too!"
definitions.example: Snap! We've both got pink buckets and spades.

definitions.definition: Ritual utterance of agreement (after the cry in the card game snap).
Used in place of expletive to express surprise, usually in response to a negative statement or news; often used facetiously.
definitions.example: "I just ran over your phone with my car." "Oh, snap!"

definitions.definition: Ritual utterance used after something is said by two people at exactly the same time.
definitions.example: "Wasn't that John?" "Wasn't that John?" "Snap!"

definitions.definition: To slacken.
To mitigate; to reduce the strength of.
To lose cohesion or solidity by a chemical combination with water; to slake.
definitions.example: Lime slacks.

definitions.definition: Old-fashioned, out of date
The study of art or beauty.
That which appeals to the senses.
The artistic motifs defining a collection of things, especially works of art; more broadly, their vibe.
definitions.example: Her most recent works have this quirky, half-serious 90's teen culture-inspired aesthetic.

definitions.definition: Concerned with beauty, artistic impact, or appearance.
Beautiful or appealing to one's sense of beauty and/or art.
definitions.example: It works well enough, but the shabby exterior offends his aesthetic sensibilities.

definitions.definition: A dish made with beaten eggs cooked in a frying pan without stirring, flipped over to cook on both sides, and sometimes filled or topped with cheese, chives or other foodstuffs.
A form of shellcode that searches the address space for multiple small blocks of data ("eggs") and recombines them into a larger block to be executed.
Any smell, whether fragrant or offensive.
A strong, pervasive quality.
Esteem.
Something which produces a scent; incense, a perfume.
Endorsement; approval.
definitions.example: We can start as soon as we get the OK.

definitions.definition: To approve.
definitions.example: I don't want to OK this amount of money.

definitions.definition: To confirm by activating a button marked OK.
All right, permitted.

definitions.definition: Satisfactory, reasonably good; not exceptional.
definitions.example: The soup was OK, but the dessert was excellent.

definitions.definition: In good health or a good emotional state.
definitions.example: He's not feeling well now, but he should be OK after some rest.

definitions.definition: Satisfactorily, sufficiently well.
definitions.example: The team did OK in the playoffs.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate acknowledgement or acceptance.
definitions.example: I promise to give it back. – OK.

definitions.definition: Used to introduce a sentence in order to draw attention to the importance of what is being said.
definitions.example: OK, I'm thinking of a number…

definitions.definition: Used in turn-taking, serving as a request to the speaker to grant the turn to the interrupter.
definitions.example: You always do this to me! When we were at your mother’s, you said that…  – OK, OK, …

definitions.definition: The chemical element (symbol O) with an atomic number of 8 and relative atomic mass of 15.9994. It is a colorless and odorless gas.
Molecular oxygen (O2), a colorless, odorless gas at room temperature, also called dioxygen.
A mixture of oxygen and other gases, administered to a patient to help them breathe.
An atom of this element.
To poke or thrust abruptly, or to make such a motion.
To deliver a quick punch.
To give someone an injection

A method of self-defence established in Japan emphasizing “jū” (soft or gentle) “jutsu” (art or technique). Japanese martial art that utilizes a large variety of techniques in defense against an opponent.
Brazilian jiu-jitsu, a related art, derived from judo.
Precision maneuvering.
A physical object, something that can be touched.
Real or concrete results.

definitions.definition: Touchable; able to be touched or felt; perceptible by the sense of touch
Possible to be treated as fact; real or concrete.
Comprehensible by the mind; understandable.
A box into which mail is put
A folder or account for the storage of e-mail; an electronic in-box or mailstore.
To pour water into the soil surrounding (plants).
To wet or supply with water; to moisten; to overflow with water; to irrigate.
To provide (animals) with water for drinking.

definitions.definition: To get or take in water.

definitions.definition: To urinate onto.
To dilute.

definitions.definition: To overvalue (securities), especially through deceptive accounting.
To fill with or secrete water.

A hide of a cow.
Any quantity of hides of cows.
Leather made from the hide of cows.
A coarse whip made of untanned leather.
To flog with a cowhide.
Gossypium, a genus of plant used as a source of cotton fiber.
Any plant that encases its seed in a thin fiber that is harvested and used as a fabric or cloth.
Any fiber similar in appearance and use to Gossypium fiber.
The textile made from the fiber harvested from a cotton plant, especially Gossypium.
An item of clothing made from cotton.
To provide with cotton.
To make or become cotton-like
To protect from harsh stimuli, coddle, or muffle.
To rub or burnish with cotton.
Made of cotton.
(often followed by "in"): To yield to a temptation or desire.

definitions.definition: To satisfy the wishes or whims of.
definitions.example: Grandma indulges the kids with sweets.

definitions.definition: To give way to (a habit or temptation); not to oppose or restrain.
definitions.example: to indulge sloth, pride, selfishness, or inclinations

definitions.definition: To grant an extension to the deadline of a payment.
To grant as by favour; to bestow in concession, or in compliance with a wish or request.
(baby-talk) Urine.
(baby-talk) The genitalia (as used for urination), especially the penis.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
That kisses.
Just touching.
A bitter, destructive feud, normally between two families, clans or factions, in which each injury or slaying is revenged: a blood feud.
(often preceded by personal) A motivational grudge against a person or faction, which may or may not be reciprocated; the state of having it in for someone.
Relating to, or characteristic of life.
definitions.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

definitions.definition: Necessary to the continuation of life; being the seat of life; being that on which life depends.
definitions.example: The brain is a vital organ.

definitions.definition: Invigorating or life-giving.
Necessary to continued existence.
definitions.example: The transition to farming was vital for the creation of civilisation.

definitions.definition: Relating to the recording of life events.
definitions.example: Birth, marriage and death certificates are vital records.

definitions.definition: Very important.
definitions.example: It is vital that you don't forget to do your homework.

definitions.definition: Containing life; living.
Capable of living; in a state to live; viable.
Large and impressive, in size, scope or extent
Pompous or pretentious
To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To lower (something) into water; to thrust or plunge under liquid and suddenly withdraw.
To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.
To enter a place for a short moment.

definitions.definition: To pronounce sentence or judgment on; to condemn.

definitions.definition: To destine; to fix irrevocably the ill fate of.
To judge; to estimate or determine as a judge.

Assured to suffer death, failure, or a similarly negative outcome.
A machine for washing dishes.
Someone who washes dishes, especially one hired to wash dishes in a restaurant.
A European bird, the wagtail.
A bird, the restless flycatcher (Myiagra inquieta).
A circumstance in which a choice must be made between two or more alternatives that seem equally undesirable.
A difficult circumstance or problem.
A type of syllogism of the form "if A is true then B is true; if C is true then D is true; either A or C is true; therefore either B or D is true".
Offering to an opponent a choice between two (equally unfavorable) alternatives.
(of a married person) To run away from home with a paramour.

To run away from home (for any reason).
The result or outcome of a cause.

definitions.definition: Impression left on the mind; sensation produced.
Execution; performance; realization; operation.
An illusion produced by technical means (as in "special effect")

definitions.definition: (sound engineering) An alteration, or device for producing an alteration, in sound after it has been produced by an instrument.
definitions.example: I just bought a couple of great effects.

definitions.definition: (etc.) A scientific phenomenon, usually named after its discoverer.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Belongings, usually as personal effects.
Consequence intended; purpose; meaning; general intent; with to.
Reality; actual meaning; fact, as distinguished from mere appearance.
Manifestation; expression; sign.
To make or bring about; to implement.
definitions.example: The best way to effect change is to work with existing stakeholders.

definitions.definition: A reserve or substitute.
definitions.example: If the goalkeeper is injured, we have a backup.

definitions.definition: A copy of a file or record, stored separately from the original, that can be used to recover the original if it is destroyed or damaged.
definitions.example: After the power failure, we had to restore the database from backup.

definitions.definition: An accumulation of material caused by a (partial) obstruction or (complete) blockage of the flow or movement of the material, or an accumulation of material that causes an overflow due to the flow being greater than the maximum possible flow.
definitions.example: The accident caused a mile-long backup on the highway.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) reinforcements
definitions.example: He's got a gun! You'd better send for backup.

definitions.definition: The area where a land battle is or was fought, which is not necessarily a field.
definitions.example: 1886 The night of the 16th of May found McPherson's command bivouacked from two to six miles west of the battlefield, along the line of the road to Vicksburg — Ulysses S. Grant, Personal memoirs of U.S. Grant, Chapter 35.

definitions.definition: The style in which someone's hair has been cut and arranged.
Conditions suitable for an organism or population of organisms to live.
definitions.example: This park offers important amphibian habitat and breeding area.

definitions.definition: A place or type of site where an organism or population naturally occurs.
A terrestrial or aquatic area distinguished by geographic, abiotic and biotic features, whether entirely natural or semi-natural.
A place in which a person lives.
A miniature man, once imagined by spermists to be present in human sperm.
The nerve map of the human body that exists on the parietal lobe of the human brain.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
Hospitable.
To increase the defenses of; to strengthen and secure by military works; to render defensible against an attack by hostile forces.
To impart strength or vigor to.
To add spirits to wine to increase the alcohol content.
definitions.example: Sherry is made by fortifying wine.

definitions.definition: To increase the nutritional value of food by adding ingredients.
definitions.example: Soy milk is often fortified with calcium.

definitions.definition: A limb or appendage that an animal uses for support or locomotion.
definitions.example: {{ux|en|Insects have six legs.}

definitions.definition: In humans, the lower limb extending from the groin to the ankle.
definitions.example: Dan won't be able to come to the party, since he broke his leg last week and is now on crutches.

definitions.definition: The portion of the lower limb of a human that extends from the knee to the ankle.
A part of garment, such as a pair of trousers/pants, that covers a leg.
definitions.example: The left leg of these jeans has a tear.

definitions.definition: A rod-like protrusion from an inanimate object, supporting it from underneath.
definitions.example: the legs of a chair or table

definitions.definition: Something that supports.
definitions.example: This observation is an important leg of my argument.

definitions.definition: A stage of a journey, race etc.
definitions.example: After six days, we're finally in the last leg of our cross-country trip.

definitions.definition: A distance that a sailing vessel does without changing the sails from one side to the other.
One side of a multiple-sided (often triangular) course in a sailing race.
A single game or match played in a tournament or other sporting contest.
One of the two sides of a right triangle that is not the hypotenuse.
One of the branches of a hyperbola or other curve which extend outward indefinitely.
(usually used in plural) The ability of something to persist or succeed over a long period of time.
definitions.example: This proposal has no legs. Almost everyone opposes it.

definitions.definition: A disreputable sporting character; a blackleg.

An extension of a steam boiler downward, in the form of a narrow space between vertical plates, sometimes nearly surrounding the furnace and ash pit, and serving to support the boiler; called also water leg.
In a grain elevator, the case containing the lower part of the belt which carries the buckets.
Denotes the half of the field on the same side as the batsman's legs; the left side for a right-handed batsman.
definitions.example: Ponsonby-Smythe hit a thumping drive through the leg fielders.

definitions.definition: A branch or lateral circuit connecting an instrument with the main line.
A branch circuit; one phase of a polyphase system.
An underlying instrument of a derivatives strategy.
An army soldier assigned to a paratrooper unit who has not yet been qualified as a paratrooper.
A gesture of submission; a bow or curtsey. Chiefly in phrase make a leg.
To remove the legs from an animal carcass.
To build legs onto a platform or stage for support.
To put a series of three or more options strikes into the stock market.
To apply force using the leg (as in 'to leg a horse').
(systems theory) A synonym of binding energy.
The cooperation of two or more nerves, muscles, organs, etc.

definitions.definition: The combined action of two or more drugs where the effects are stronger than their mere sum.
Benefits resulting from combining different groups, people, objects or processes.
